insight: Iran's performance in the 2026 World Cup has shown a notable trend when it comes to hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum after both of their breaks so far. This means that after taking a brief pause to rehydrate, Iran has been able to increase their pace and intensity, which can be a significant advantage in a match. The data suggests that this momentum boost is not dependent on their strategy before the break, as they have only taken breaks when not pressing, and in both cases, they have managed to maintain a positive change in momentum, averaging a gain of 0.200 minutes. This ability to refocus and come back stronger after a hydration break could be a key factor in Iran's success in the tournament, allowing them to push through challenging periods and capitalize on scoring opportunities.
